LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FRANK MYERS'S SECOND AMENDED MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION APPOINTING RECEIVER AND FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 12, 2012. THE COURT WILL APPOINT A RECEIVER LIMITED TO THE FOLLOWING: A) TO INSURE THAT THE PROPERTY IS FREE OF NOTICE OF VIOLATION FROM THE CITY AND B) TO MAKE SURE THAT ALL INSURANCE AND TAXES ARE PAID WHEN THEY COME DUE. THE RECEIVER WILL BE APPOINTED EFFECTIVE SEPT. 14. HOWEVER, THAT APPOINTMENT WILL NOT TAKE EFFECT IF AT THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 12 AT 9:30 A.M., THE OWNER HAS SHOWN THAT THEY HAVE CURED ALL NOTICES OF VIOLATION AND THE INSURANCE AND TAXES HAVE BEEN PAID IN FULL. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976 =(302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS JENNY LIN AND FRANCISCAN HOTEL, LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION BY JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED. BY GENERALLY APPEARING IN THIS ACTION, DEFENDANTS WAIVED ANY ARGUMENTS REGARDING DEFECTIVE SERVICE. SEE CCP 410.50 ("A GENERAL APPEARANCE BY A PARTY IS EQUIVALENT TO PERSONAL SERVICE OF SUMMONS ON SUCH PARTY."). SEE ALSO, FACTOR HEALTH MANAGEMENT, LLC V. SUPERIOR COURT, 132 CAL.APP.4TH 246, 251 (2005)("A MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION "INVOLVE[S] A DETERMINATION RELATED TO THE MERITS OF THE CASE'."); 366-386 GEARY ST., L.P. V. SUPERIOR COURT, 219 CAL.APP.3D 1186, 1194 (1990); FIREMAN'S FUND INS. CO. V. SPARKS CONST., INC., 114 CAL. APP. 4TH 1135, 1145 (2004). DEFENDANTS LITIGATED A MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AS WELL AS OTHER MOTIONS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF FRANK MYERS' MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S DEPOSITION AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS - THE COURT ADOPTS THE RECOMMENDATION OF PRO TEM KANE AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THE COURT PREVIOUSLY RULED THAT DEFENDANT GENERALLY APPEARED ON JUNE 8, 2012. SINCE DEFENDANT WAS SERVED ON JUNE 15, 2012 FOR A DEPOSITION ON JUNE 21, 2012, THE NOTICE WAS NOT TIMELY BECAUSE UNDER CCP 2025.210(B), SERVICE OF A DEPOSITION NOTICE ON A DEFENDANT CANNOT BE MADE UNTIL 20 DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR APPEARANCE ABSENT AN ORDER SHORTENING TIME. (SEE O'GRADY V. SUPERIOR COURT (2006) 139 CAL.APP.4TH 1423, 1454 [REQUIRED TO SECURE ORDER TO CONDUCT DISCOVERY PRIOR TO 20 DAY PERIOD]; BERNSON V. BROWNING-FERRIS INDUSTRIES (1994) 7 CAL.4TH 926, 931 N.2 [TO THE SAME EFFECT].) SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Plaintiff Frank Myers' Motion To Compel Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-10-2012 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALI ARI AALAEI, TEL. NO. 357-3600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Frank Myers' Motion To Compel
Defendant's Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions - The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Kane as follows: Denied. The court previously ruled that defendant generally appeared on June 8, 2012. Since defendant was served on June 15, 2012 for a deposition on June 21, 2012, the notice was not timely because under CCP 2025.210(b), service of a deposition notice on a defendant cannot be made until 20 days after service of summons or appearance absent an order shortening time. (See O'Grady v. Superior Court (2006) 139 Cal.App.4th 1423, 1454 [required to secure order to conduct discovery prior to 20 day period]; Bernson v. Browning-Ferris Industries (1994) 7 Cal.4th 926, 931 n.2 [to the same effect].) Sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

ATTY. BO ZENG, TEL. NO. 357-3600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS JENNY LIN AND FRANCISCAN HOTEL, LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION BY JUDGE CURTIS KARNOW. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: Motion is Denied. By generally appearing in this action, Defendants waived any arguments regarding defective service. See CCP 410.50 ("A general appearance by a party is equivalent to personal service of summons on such party."). See also, Factor Health Management, LLC v. Superior Court, 132 Cal.App.4th 246, 251 (2005)("a motion for a preliminary injunction "involve[s] a determination related to the merits of the case'."); 366-386 Geary St., L.P. v. Superior Court, 219 Cal.App.3d 1186, 1194 (1990); Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Sparks Const., Inc., 114 Cal. App. 4th 1135, 1145 (2004). Defendants litigated a motion for preliminary injunction as well as other motions. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976
